title: aragallus bigelowii
intro: Aragallus bigelowii, also known as Bigelow's senna, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). The medicinal properties of Aragallus bigelowii are attributed to its high concentration of anthraquinones, which have been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of this plant include relief from constipation, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Aragallus bigelowii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried extract for medicinal purposes, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential side effects and interactions.
